summary: Matched 11 previously stubbed item functions across Lugia, Old Kuri, White Bea, Ness Bat, Ness PK Thunder Ball, Matadogas, Hitodeman, Great Fox Laser, and Kirby item files. The PR also refined item attribute/item-var structs and two item headers to expose fields and function signatures needed by the matched bodies.

key_files.role: Shared item attribute/item-var struct definitions expanded for Lugia, Hitodeman, and White Bea fields used by matched item functions.
Matched itLugia_UnkMotion3_Phys and it_802D23F4 using Lugia attributes, life timer setup, hitlag callbacks, and item physics transitions.
Updated recovered prototype for it_802D1830 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void it_802D1830(Item_GObj* gobj).
Matched it_802D4494 and added local attribute fields x14/x18 plus use of hitodeman item-var x84.
Updated recovered prototype for it_802D4494 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void it_802D4494(Item_GObj* gobj).
Matched it_802E4558 and itWhitebea_UnkMotion7_Anim, including a local declaration for grIceMt_801FA6D8 and White Bea attribute x14 use.
Matched it_802AB3F0 with null checks, Vec3 zeroing, and indexed pointer adjustment before reading pkthunder item vars.
Matched it_802EB1EC and added include for it_2725.h.
Matched it_802F2810 and added item-related includes.
Matched it_802CB798 with life timer initialization, animation update, hitlag callbacks, and setup call.
Matched it_2725_Logic58_PickedUp using OR-combined itcmd vars to select an animation update mode, then Item_802694CC.
Matched itOldkuri_UnkMotion1_Phys with collision/transition check and velocity/floor-normal helpers.

what_changed: Replaced 11 function stubs with C bodies listed in the PR body and confirmed by the bot report as 100.00% after the PR.
Expanded itLugiaAttributes with x8, xC, and x10 fields and itLugia_ItemVars with x60 plus adjusted padding.
Expanded itHitodeman_ItemVars with x84 and adjusted padding; local Hitodeman attributes gained x14 and x18 fields used in a random range calculation.
Expanded itWhiteBeaAttributes with padding and x14 used by itWhitebea_UnkMotion7_Anim.
Updated item headers for recovered function signatures in ithitodeman.h and itlugia.h.
Added item-related includes in itgreatfoxlaser.c and itkirby_2F23.c and introduced a local declaration for grIceMt_801FA6D8 in itwhitebea.c.

smart_moves.text: The PR paired function matches with the minimal struct-field exposure needed to express real item and attribute accesses instead of leaving all access as opaque padding.
Recovered signatures were propagated to owning headers where shown by the diff, avoiding stale UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for touched functions.
The PR body's 100% match claim is supported by the decomp-dev bot report listing 11 new 100.00% item matches and +1336 matched-code bytes.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For item matches, recovered bodies may reveal only narrow pieces of an item-var or attribute struct; exposing those concrete fields while preserving padding can be enough to make small functions readable and matchable.
When a matched body proves a function signature, update the corresponding item header rather than leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS in place.

Similar item setup routines can share a pattern: set xD44_lifeTimer from attributes, call it_80274740, start an item animation with Item_80268E5C, assign efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll hitlag callbacks, then call it_8026B3A8.

naming_conventions.convention: The PR preserves address-style names such as it_802D23F4, it_802CB798, and it_802AB3F0 when semantics are not provided by the evidence, while using existing descriptive names such as itLugia_UnkMotion3_Phys and itWhitebea_UnkMotion7_Anim.
Local item object variables are consistently named gobj for the item GObj and ip for the Item pointer in the matched bodies.
Newly identified but semantically unnamed struct members remain offset-style names such as x10, x14, x18, x60, and x84.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used PAD_STACK in several newly matched functions with small stack padding amounts, including 8-byte and 16-byte pads.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR evidence shows successful bot matching but does not explain why each PAD_STACK is required.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: For it_802AB3F0, the matched code uses explicit null-check nesting, manual Vec3 zeroing in z/y/x order, and byte-pointer indexing by idx * 0x18 before reading pkthunder item vars.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: This is evidence of the accepted PR shape, not a global recommendation to prefer raw pointer arithmetic when a stronger type is available.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: For it_2725_Logic58_PickedUp, OR-combining four itcmd vars creates a compact zero/nonzero test before selecting Item_80268E5C mode 1 or 0.
For itLugia_UnkMotion3_Phys, a post-decrement item-var counter gates velocity.z assignment versus zeroing and transition to it_802D1830.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report listed matched code at 56.03% (+0.03%, +1336 bytes), 11 new matches, each moving from 0.00% to 100.00%, plus one .sdata2 improvement in ithitodeman.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching it_802AB3F0 or pkthunder xDEC to see whether the raw idx * 0x18 pointer arithmetic was replaced by a stronger type.
Find later PRs touching grIceMt_801FA6D8 declarations to see whether the local declaration in itwhitebea.c moved to an owning header.
Search item setup functions using xD44_lifeTimer, Item_80268E5C, efLib_PauseAll, efLib_ResumeAll, and it_8026B3A8 for reusable setup patterns.
Search later changes to itLugiaAttributes, itWhiteBeaAttributes, and itHitodemanAttributes for semantic field renames of x10, x14, x18, x60, and x84.
Search PAD_STACK uses in item matches to identify which ones were later eliminated or documented with objdiff evidence.

notes: The PR body and bot report both identify the same 11 matched functions, and the diff shows the corresponding stub replacements.
There are no human review comments in the loaded slice, so reviewer intent or requested changes cannot be inferred.
The bot report supports 100.00% matches for the listed functions, but no local build command, objdiff command, or author rationale is included beyond the bot summary.
PAD_STACK use is visible in the diff; the loaded evidence does not independently justify each stack pad beyond the reported matches.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Item function matches may require narrow struct-field recovery
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2271 matched multiple item functions by exposing specific item-var and attribute fields in itCommonItems.h while preserving surrounding padding.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recovered item signatures should be propagated to headers
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2271 updated item headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures where the touched bodies proved them.

source_update_candidates.title: Require explicit rationale for PAD_STACK retained in matched item functions
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2271 shows several successful item matches using PAD_STACK, but the slice lacks per-function rationale. Existing standards already say matching tactics need evidence; this PR can be cited as a reminder to keep PAD_STACK evidence narrow and documented when curating examples.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential standards example only; do not treat this PR as blanket approval for PAD_STACK because the loaded evidence does not explain necessity.

source_update_candidates.title: Item setup pattern across Lugia and Matadogas matched functions
source_update_candidates.text: In PR #2271, it_802D23F4 in itlugia.c and it_802CB798 in itmatadogas.c share a setup sequence involving xD44_lifeTimer from attributes, it_80274740, Item_80268E5C with ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE, efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll callbacks, and it_8026B3A8.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could help future item-function matching searches, but should be curated as a local pattern rather than a global rule.

source_update_candidates.title: White Bea item code locally declared grIceMt_801FA6D8 in PR #2271
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2271 added void grIceMt_801FA6D8(void); in itwhitebea.c and calls it from it_802E4558 after item state/audio/animation setup.
source_update_candidates.reason: May indicate a missing owning header or stage/item cross-call fact for curator review; the PR slice does not prove intended long-term declaration ownership.

rejection_notes: Do not promote raw pointer arithmetic in it_802AB3F0 as preferred style; the diff shows it matched, but current standards prefer typed fields when available.
Do not infer semantic names for x10, x14, x18, x60, x84, or other offset-style fields from this PR alone.
Do not claim human review approval or requested changes; no human review comments were loaded.
Do not claim broad data-section work from the .sdata2 bot improvement; the diff and PR body are focused on code matches and small struct/header changes.
